content.full_text:
January 30, 2013


Blaise J. Jackson
General Counsel
Law Offices of Jeffrey G. Scott
16935 West Bernardo Drive, Suite 170
San Diego, CA 92127

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. 13-001

Dear Mr. Jackson:

       This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of members of the Palo Verde
Healthcare District Board regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").¹ The Fair Political Practices Commission does not provide advice relating to
past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), enclosed.) It has come to our attention that the Palo
Verde Healthcare District Board voted on the matters you presented in your request for advice.
Additionally, we do not offer an advice regarding laws not within our jurisdiction, including
Government Code Section 1090.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

                                                           Sincerely,

                                                           Zackery P. Morazzini
                                                           General Counsel


                                                      By:   Heather M. Rowan
HMR:jgl                                                    Counsel, Legal Division

_______________
       ¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether members of the Palo Verde Healthcare District Board had conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding certain matters that came before the board.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the board had already voted on the matters in question, as the FPPC does not provide advice on past conduct per Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), and the matters also involved Government Code Section 1090 which is outside FPPC jurisdiction.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The legal question and FPPC's reasoning are embedded in the body of the letter. Document clearly states two bases for declination: (1) matters already voted on (past conduct), and (2) involvement of Government Code Section 1090 (outside FPPC jurisdiction).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether members of the Palo Verde Healthcare District Board had conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding certain matters that came before the board.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the board had already voted on the matters in question, as the FPPC does not provide advice on past conduct per Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), and the matters also involved Government Code Section 1090 which is outside FPPC jurisdiction.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide an advice letter to the Palo Verde Healthcare District Board regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, citing that the board had already voted on the matters and the FPPC does not advise on past conduct or laws outside its jurisdiction.
